message:
fix: normalize bare official OpenAI/Cohere origins to /v1 endpoints and always persist thinking level

Two independent fixes:

1. providers/mod.rs: normalize_openai_base() and
   normalize_openai_responses_base() treated the bare official origin
   https://api.openai.com like a generic proxy, yielding
   /chat/completions or /responses without the /v1 prefix. Similarly,
   normalize_cohere_base() didn't special-case https://api.cohere.com.
   Add origin-specific guards that route official bare URLs to the
   correct /v1 (OpenAI) and /v2 (Cohere) endpoints while preserving
   existing proxy behavior. Add regression tests for each case.

2. rpc.rs: always apply_thinking_level() on set_model even when the
   clamped level matches the current runtime level, because the session
   header still needs the level persisted for session reload. Add two
   new RPC integration tests: one verifying the header persists when
   thinking is already at the target level, and one verifying the
   clamped-to-off transition is recorded when switching to a
   non-thinking provider.

Track new issue bd-6l1hz for the OpenAI origin normalization bug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-6koq","title":"Phase 2: Shared Mock Infrastructure for Both Runtimes","description":"# Phase 2: Shared Mock Infrastructure for Both Runtimes

## Purpose
For differential testing, BOTH runtimes must use IDENTICAL mock infrastructure. If the TS side uses one HTTP mock and the Rust side uses another, outputs will differ even if the extension code is correct. We need a shared, deterministic mock layer.

## Why Shared Mocks Are Critical
Extensions interact with the outside world through hostcalls:
- HTTP requests (pi.http / fetch)
- Command execution (pi.exec)
- Session state (pi.session.getMessages, etc.)
- UI rendering (pi.ui.showSpinner, etc.)
- Filesystem (reading/writing files)

In production, these have non-deterministic results. For testing, BOTH runtimes must return the SAME mock responses for the SAME inputs.

## Architecture Decision: Mock Specification Files
Rather than implementing mocks in both TS and Rust separately, we define mocks in a SHARED JSON specification:

{
  "http_mocks": [
    {"method": "GET", "url": "https://example.com", "response": {"status": 200, "body": "ok"}}
  ],
  "exec_mocks": [
    {"command": "echo", "args": ["hello"], "result": {"stdout": "hello\n", "exit_code": 0}}
  ],
  "session_state": {
    "messages": [...],
    "name": "test-session",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
  },
  "ui_capture": true
}

Both runtimes read the SAME spec file and return the SAME responses.

## Mock Categories
1. HTTP: Deterministic responses for any URL the extension might hit
2. Exec: Deterministic command output for any command the extension might run
3. Session: Pre-defined message history and session state
4. UI: Capture all UI calls (spinners, progress, dialogs) without rendering
5. Filesystem: Sandboxed temp directory with predefined contents
6. Events: Standard event payloads for tool_call, tool_result, turn_start, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mock spec format documented and validated
- TS runtime reads mock spec and returns correct responses
- Rust runtime reads mock spec and returns correct responses
- Both runtimes produce byte-identical output for simple extension + mock spec","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:58.324908781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T20:55:12.613415542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T20:55:12.613348538Z","close_reason":"Child tasks complete: bd-1yfi (mock spec JSON schema + fixtures), bd-1l8n (TS oracle harness reading the same mock spec), bd-1bje (Rust ConformanceMockSession/dispatcher reading the same mock spec), bd-1696 (standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes). Shared deterministic mocks are now in place for differential TS↔Rust conformance.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6koq","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:15Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3980,"issue_id":"bd-6koq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN DECISION: Why JSON mock spec files instead of code-level mocking?

1. PORTABILITY: Same mock spec used by both TS and Rust runtimes. No risk of mock divergence.
2. DECLARATIVE: Easy to understand, review, and modify without code changes.
3. REPRODUCIBLE: Same spec file = same behavior = same outputs. Always.
4. EXTENSIBLE: Add new mock categories by extending the JSON schema.

Alternative considered: code-level mocking in each runtime separately. Rejected because: two different mock implementations could have subtle behavioral differences that cause false conformance failures. The whole POINT is to isolate extension behavior differences from infrastructure differences.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:16Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-6l1hz","title":"Normalize bare official OpenAI origins to /v1 endpoints","description":"Fresh-eyes audit of src/providers/mod.rs found that normalize_openai_base() and normalize_openai_responses_base() treat the bare official origin https://api.openai.com like a generic OpenAI-compatible proxy base. That yields https://api.openai.com/chat/completions or https://api.openai.com/responses instead of the real official /v1 endpoints. Built-in registry entries already use /v1, so the bug is easy to miss, but custom model entries using the bare official origin are routed incorrectly. Special-case the official origin while preserving existing proxy behavior, and add focused regression coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T09:53:32.287689351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T09:53:43.348122351Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6mwn3","title":"FUZZ-P3.1: GitHub Actions workflow for continuous fuzzing (30-60s per target per CI run)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"rosepond","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:03:05.241116591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:39:47.750697996Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:39:47.750674272Z","close_reason":"Added .github/workflows/fuzz.yml with quick/nightly fuzz jobs, per-target 60s/1800s runs, crash artifact publication, and machine-readable run summaries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","fuzz","github-actions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6mwn3","depends_on_id":"bd-1uny7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6mwn3","depends_on_id":"bd-291y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6mwn3","depends_on_id":"bd-5h0cd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3701,"issue_id":"bd-6mwn3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-P3.1: GitHub Actions Continuous Fuzzing Workflow

### Why CI Fuzzing?
Local fuzzing finds bugs when developers remember to run it. CI fuzzing runs automatically on every push, catching regressions and exploring new code paths as the codebase evolves. Even 30-60 seconds per target per CI run accumulates significant fuzzing coverage over time.

### Workflow Design

```yaml
# .github/workflows/fuzz.yml
name: Fuzz Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *'  # Nightly deep runs

jobs:
  fuzz-quick:
    # Runs on every push/PR — 30-60 seconds per target
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        target:
          - fuzz_sse_parser
          - fuzz_sse_stream
          - fuzz_session_jsonl
          - fuzz_session_entry
          - fuzz_provider_event
          - fuzz_message_deser
          - fuzz_message_roundtrip
          - fuzz_config
          - fuzz_tool_paths
          - fuzz_grep_pattern
          - fuzz_extension_payload
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
      - run: cargo install cargo-fuzz
      - run: cargo fuzz run ${{ matrix.target }} -- -max_total_time=60
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: failure()
        with:
          name: crash-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: fuzz/artifacts/${{ matrix.target }}/

  fuzz-nightly:
    # Runs on schedule — 30 minutes per target
    if: github.event_name == 'schedule'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        target: [fuzz_sse_parser, fuzz_provider_event, fuzz_session_jsonl, fuzz_message_deser]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
      - run: cargo install cargo-fuzz
      # Restore corpus from cache for continuous coverage accumulation
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: fuzz/corpus/${{ matrix.target }}
          key: fuzz-corpus-${{ matrix.target }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: fuzz-corpus-${{ matrix.target }}-
      - run: cargo fuzz run ${{ matrix.target }} -- -max_total_time=1800
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: failure()
        with:
          name: crash-nightly-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: fuzz/artifacts/${{ matrix.target }}/
```

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Quick runs (60s) on every push**: Fast enough to not slow CI, catches obvious regressions
2. **Nightly deep runs (30min) on schedule**: Explores deeper code paths, accumulates corpus
3. **Matrix strategy**: Runs all targets in parallel for fast feedback
4. **Corpus caching**: Uses GitHub Actions cache to persist corpus across runs — each nightly run starts from where the previous one left off
5. **Crash artifact upload**: Failing runs upload the crash input for debugging
6. **P0 targets in nightly**: Only the highest-priority targets get extended nightly runs to save CI minutes

### Cost Considerations
- Quick runs: ~11 targets × 60s = ~11 minutes of CI time per push
- Nightly runs: ~4 targets × 30min = ~2 hours per night
- GitHub Actions free tier: 2000 min/month → sufficient for moderate push frequency
- Optimization: Only run fuzz targets on pushes that touch relevant source files (path filter)

### Prerequisites
- All P2 harnesses must be complete and passing
- Seed corpora must be committed to the repository
- Rust nightly must be available in CI

### Files to Create
- .github/workflows/fuzz.yml

### Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow runs successfully on push to main
- Matrix parallelism works for all targets
- Corpus caching works across nightly runs
- Crash artifacts are uploaded on failure
- Total quick-run CI time < 15 minutes","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:03:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6q51","title":"Trace remaining extension E2E modules in matrix","description":"Add remaining extension-centric E2E test modules to docs/traceability_matrix.json and validate governance/staleness checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:55:29.693744331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:57:22.325410891Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T09:57:22.325389050Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6vcm","title":"Feature: Extension Conformance Testing","description":"# Feature: Extension Conformance Testing

## Overview

Create a comprehensive conformance testing suite that verifies the Rust extension runtime behaves identically to the TypeScript reference implementation.

## Why This Matters

Extensions are a critical API surface. Third-party extensions must work identically in Rust and TypeScript. Conformance testing is the only way to guarantee 100% API compatibility.

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Reference Capture (TypeScript)
Run test extensions against pi-mono (TypeScript) and capture:
- Hostcall request/response pairs
- Event handler invocations
- Tool registration results
- Error cases and messages

### 2. Fixture-Based Testing (Rust)
Replay the same scenarios in Rust and compare:
- Same inputs produce same outputs
- Same events fire at same points
- Same errors for invalid operations

### 3. Property-Based Testing (proptest)
Verify invariants hold for all inputs:
- Hostcalls never panic
- Invalid inputs produce errors
- State consistency maintained

## Test Categories

| Category | Test Cases | Purpose |
|----------|------------|---------|
| Hostcall Tool | 10+ | Register, execute, parameters, errors |
| Hostcall Exec | 8+ | Run command, capture output, exit codes |
| Hostcall Http | 12+ | GET/POST, headers, timeouts, errors |
| Hostcall Session | 13+ | Get messages, append, fork |
| Hostcall UI | 15+ | Spinners, progress, notifications |
| Hostcall Events | 10+ | Subscribe, unsubscribe, dispatch |
| Event tool_call | 5+ | Blocking, non-blocking, errors |
| Event tool_result | 5+ | Modification, pass-through |
| Event lifecycle | 10+ | turn_start/end, agent_start/end |

## Fixture Format

```json
{
    "suite": "hostcall-tool",
    "runtime": "typescript",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "cases": [
        {
            "name": "register_tool_basic",
            "input": { "type": "tool", "method": "register", "args": {...} },
            "expected": { "success": true, "data": null }
        }
    ]
}
```

## Directory Structure

```
tests/conformance/
├── mod.rs                    # Test runner
├── fixtures/
│   ├── ts_outputs/          # From TypeScript capture
│   └── snapshots/           # Insta snapshots
├── ts_reference/
│   ├── capture.ts           # Reference capture harness
│   └── test-extensions/     # Test extensions
└── proptest.rs              # Property-based tests
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Reference capture program for TypeScript
- [ ] 100+ fixture files generated from pi-mono
- [ ] Rust tests replay all fixtures
- [ ] 100% pass rate on fixtures
- [ ] Property tests for invariants (1000+ cases)
- [ ] CI integration with detailed JSONL logging
- [ ] Conformance report generation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:09:08.853704645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:50.177975575Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:50.177895195Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6vcm","depends_on_id":"bd-30zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:15Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3993,"issue_id":"bd-6vcm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2 progress: Wired missing set_label into dispatch_session. Added 28 new dispatcher conformance tests (61 total, was 33). Coverage now: Tool=9, Session=23, Exec=4, HTTP=4, UI=8, Events=5, Infra=3. Also found+fixed: EditTool camelCase params, events list needs extension registration.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:13:24Z"},{"id":3994,"issue_id":"bd-6vcm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All conformance test category targets met: Tool 10+, Exec 8+, HTTP 12+, Session 20+, UI 15+, Events 10+. Total: 89 dispatcher tests + 6 crash recovery tests = 95 conformance tests. Plus 61 dispatcher tests from earlier sessions and property-based tests.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:41Z"}]}

@@ -186,6 +186,15 @@ fn normalize_openai_base_appends_endpoint() {
             "https://api.openai.com/v1",
             "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions",
         ),
+        // Bare official OpenAI origin gets /v1/chat/completions appended
+        (
+            "https://api.openai.com",
+            "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions",
+        ),
+        (
+            "https://api.openai.com/",
+            "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions",
+        ),
         // Already has /chat/completions - kept as-is
         (
             "https://custom.api.com/v1/chat/completions",
@@ -233,6 +242,15 @@ fn normalize_openai_responses_base_appends_endpoint() {
             "https://api.openai.com/v1",
             "https://api.openai.com/v1/responses",
         ),
+        // Bare official OpenAI origin gets /v1/responses appended
+        (
+            "https://api.openai.com",
+            "https://api.openai.com/v1/responses",
+        ),
+        (
+            "https://api.openai.com/",
+            "https://api.openai.com/v1/responses",
+        ),
         // /chat/completions suffix stripped, then /responses appended
         (
             "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions",
@@ -265,10 +283,10 @@ fn normalize_cohere_base_appends_endpoint() {
             "https://api.cohere.com/v2",
             "https://api.cohere.com/v2/chat",
         ),
-        // Bare URL gets /chat appended
-        ("https://api.cohere.com", "https://api.cohere.com/chat"),
-        // Trailing slash stripped, then /chat appended
-        ("https://api.cohere.com/", "https://api.cohere.com/chat"),
+        // Bare official Cohere origin gets /v2/chat appended
+        ("https://api.cohere.com", "https://api.cohere.com/v2/chat"),
+        // Trailing slash stripped, then /v2/chat appended
+        ("https://api.cohere.com/", "https://api.cohere.com/v2/chat"),
     ];
     for (input, expected) in &cases {
         let result = normalize_cohere_base(input);
